ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general oolong tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103). No specific code exists for cassia seed roasted oolong tea blend; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GE0LM covers oolong tea as a category. No specific code for cassia seed oolong blend; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers oolong tea as a substance. No specific code for cassia seed oolong blend; closest_parent used.

main_ingredients: cassia seeds (Semen Cassiae / Jue Ming Zi)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 ml (one cup) of brewed cassia seed oolong tea, as typically served at a Taiwanese tea house.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA SR Legacy 14355) and Cassia seeds compositional data (USDA FoodData Central / peer-reviewed phytochemical literature)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Cassia seed roasted oolong tea is a specialty blend not individually listed in any approved primary database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917) as the primary base. Cassia seeds contribute negligible additional macronutrients to the brewed beverage at typical infusion concentrations; the brewed liquid profile is dominated by the tea base. Values reflect the brewed beverage as consumed, not the dry seed or leaf.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — plain brewed tea analogue; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cassia seed roasted oolong tea. Plain brewed tea (including oolong) contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates and is classified as effectively zero-GI. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention and Atkinson et al. (2008), unsweetened brewed teas are assigned GI of 0 (effectively non-glycemic). Per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (lowest valid positive value) to satisfy numeric field requirements; gi_category is low; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate per serving is negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content; cassia seeds have traditional use as a mild antihypertensive botanical
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml, this unsweetened brewed tea poses negligible sodium load and may be freely consumed by individuals managing hypertension, provided it is served without added sugar or salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; cassia seeds contain anthraquinone glycosides studied for potential glucose-modulating effects
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened cassia seed oolong tea contains effectively zero glycemic carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of 0 per serving, making it a suitable beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, sweetened or milk-added versions significantly alter this profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and cholesterol; oolong tea polyphenols and cassia seed compounds have been associated with lipid-modulating properties in observational studies
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no saturated fat or dietary cholesterol, and the polyphenolic content of oolong tea has been associated with modest improvements in LDL cholesterol in some clinical studies, supporting its inclusion in a heart-healthy dietary pattern when consumed unsweetened.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cassia Seed Roasted Oolong Black Tea (决明子红乌龙茶) is a specialty herbal-botanical tea blend originating from Taiwanese tea culture and popularised in Singapore by the chain CHICHA San Chen. It is made by roasting cassia seeds (Semen Cassiae, known in traditional Chinese medicine as Jue Ming Zi, 决明子) together with oolong tea leaves to produce a full-bodied, aromatic infusion. The roasting process deepens the flavour profile, imparting a toasty, slightly nutty character with the characteristic floral and woody notes of oolong tea. Cassia seeds are the dried ripe seeds of Cassia obtusifolia or Cassia tora and have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries for their purported benefits to liver health, vision, and bowel regularity. As a brewed beverage, the drink is essentially calorie-free when served plain, containing negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate. It is naturally caffeine-containing due to the oolong tea base. The glycemic index is effectively zero, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. The sodium content is very low. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its plain form. The beverage is described as full-bodied with a unique aroma and is recommended by the restaurant as a pairing for mousse or fresh milk-based desserts. Clinically, it is a low-concern beverage for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when consumed unsweetened.

confidence_reason: This is a specialty proprietary blend (CHICHA San Chen) not individually listed in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea data (FDC ID 171917), which is the closest approved source match. The brewed beverage profile is well-supported for plain tea; cassia seed contribution to the brewed liquid macronutrient profile is negligible at typical infusion concentrations. GI is estimated by analogy from unsweetened brewed tea convention. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero for this brewed tea beverage; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea entry (FDC ID 171917). 2. fat_g is naturally zero for this brewed tea beverage; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are naturally zero as this is a plain brewed tea with no added fats. 4. sugar_g is naturally zero for unsweetened brewed tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero in the brewed liquid (fibre remains in the seed/leaf solids, not extracted into the beverage). 6. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero; plant-based beverage. 7. Vitamins B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin C, vitamin A, vitamin D are confirmed zero or below detection threshold in brewed oolong tea per USDA FoodData Central; reported as 0.0 (trace/confirmed zero). 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea entry. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea entry. 10. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for unsweetened brewed teas. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 11. This record covers the plain brewed tea as served without added sugar or milk. Sweetened or milk-added variants (e.g. with fresh milk or mousse) would have substantially different nutritional profiles. 12. Cassia seeds contain anthraquinone compounds (emodin, rhein) that may have laxative effects at high doses; the brewed beverage at normal consumption levels is generally considered safe for most adults. 13. Cuisine is classified as Chinese (Taiwanese origin, Singapore restaurant context); multilingual naming follows Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that food_group was 'Mixed' in input; corrected to 'Beverages' as this is a brewed tea. Applied zero-macro justification rule for protein_g and fat_g. Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule (GI set to 1). Populated all multilingual names per Singaporean dish rule. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases.

Iteration 2: Reviewed all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) and confirmed documentation in notes. Verified ontology match_type values are all from approved set (exact/closest_parent/none). Confirmed all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 pass.
